basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Qāf — the sūra of the opening letter — begins with an oath by “the glorious Qurʾān” and confronts the great denial of the disbelievers: resurrection. Qushayrī makes of the letter Qāf the key of the divine Names: Qawiyy, Qādir, Qadīr, Qarīb. The sūra unfolds the signs of creation — the seamless sky, the spread-out earth, the blessed water that revives the dead land — as so many proofs of the “coming forth” from the graves. It culminates on the verse of nearness: “We are nearer to him than his jugular vein,” of which Qushayrī says it bears dread for some, intimacy of heart for others. It paints the death-throes, the two recording angels, the driver and the witness, the Hell that asks “are there yet more?”, and the Garden brought near to the godfearing — promised to the “faithful returner” and to “whoever has a heart.” It is sealed on the mission of reminder without compulsion.
themes: the key of the divine Names

versets.ar: ق ۚ وَالْقُرْآنِ الْمَجِيدِ
versets.tr: qāf wa-l-qurʾāni l-majīdi
versets.trad: Qāf. By the glorious Qurʾān!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Qāf is the key of His Names: Qawiyy (the Strong), Qādir and Qadīr (the Powerful), Qarīb (the Near). He swears by these Names and by the glorious Qurʾān. The answer to the oath is implied: you will surely be resurrected; or: “what the earth diminishes of them, We know.” The isolated letter opens the sūra on the mystery of the Name and the majesty of the Word.

mots.def: opening letter, key of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

mots.racine: ق ر أ / م ج د

versets.ar: بَلْ عَجِبُوا أَن جَاءَهُم مُّنذِرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَقَالَ الْكَافِرُونَ هَٰذَا شَيْءٌ عَجِيبٌ
versets.tr: bal ʿajibū an jāʾahum mundhirun minhum fa-qāla l-kāfirūna hādhā shayʾun ʿajībun
versets.trad: They marvel that a warner from among themselves has come to them; and the disbelievers say: “This is a strange thing!
versets.commentaire: The warner (mundhir) is Muḥammad, risen from among them. Qushayrī: marveling is a way the soul expresses that it judges improbable a thing outside the ordinary, of which it had no prior knowledge. What scandalizes them is no flaw in the message, but their own inability to conceive the unheard-of.

mots.racine: ع ج ب
ن ذ ر

versets.ar: أَإِذَا مِتْنَا وَكُنَّا تُرَابًا ۖ ذَٰلِكَ رَجْعٌ بَعِيدٌ
versets.tr: a-idhā mitnā wa-kunnā turāban dhālika rajʿun baʿīdun
versets.trad: Could it be that once dead and turned to dust we shall return? That is a far-fetched return!”
versets.commentaire: “A far-fetched return” (rajʿ baʿīd): they deem it improbable to revive after dust. Qushayrī recalls that the denial of resurrection is their recurrent error — they measure God's power by their own impotence. The next verse will answer that nothing is lost of what the earth absorbs.

mots.racine: م و ت / ت ر ب
ر ج ع / ب ع د

versets.ar: قَدْ عَلِمْنَا مَا تَنقُصُ الْأَرْضُ مِنْهُمْ ۖ وَعِندَنَا كِتَابٌ حَفِيظٌ
versets.tr: qad ʿalimnā mā tanquṣu l-arḍu minhum wa-ʿindanā kitābun ḥafīẓun
versets.trad: We know what the earth takes from them, and with Us is a Book that preserves.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a consolation for the buried servant: when the earth covers him, when companions turn back, when loved ones stir at his death — who visits him, who tends him, at the edge of his grave? The Real says: “We know!” Perhaps He informs the angels: “My servant whom I drew from his world — here are his scattered limbs, his decomposed bones.” And with Us a preserving Book (kitāb ḥafīẓ): the guarded Tablet, where We have inscribed the detail of their states without any forgetting.

mots.racine: ن ق ص / أ ر ض
mots.sens: what the earth diminishes of them

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ح ف ظ

versets.ar: بَلْ كَذَّبُوا بِالْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَاءَهُمْ فَهُمْ فِي أَمْرٍ مَّرِيجٍ
versets.tr: bal kadhdhabū bi-l-ḥaqqi lammā jāʾahum fa-hum fī amrin marījin
versets.trad: Rather, they denied the Truth when it came to them, and so they are in a confused state.
versets.commentaire: “In a confused affair” (amr marīj). Qushayrī: marīj means tangled and murky: they waver in the darkness of their bewilderment, they flounder in their doubt. Having denied the Truth at the moment it presented itself leaves them without support: whoever refuses the light remains prisoner of the soul's darkness.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ح ق ق
mots.sens: they denied the Truth that came to them

mots.racine: م ر ج

versets.ar: أَفَلَمْ يَنظُرُوا إِلَى السَّمَاءِ فَوْقَهُمْ كَيْفَ بَنَيْنَاهَا وَزَيَّنَّاهَا وَمَا لَهَا مِن فُرُوجٍ
versets.tr: a-fa-lam yanẓurū ilā s-samāʾi fawqahum kayfa banaynāhā wa-zayyannāhā wa-mā lahā min furūjin
versets.trad: Do they not look at the sky above them, how We built it and adorned it, and how it has no cracks?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: do they not take heed? are they not guided by what We raised above them? We set up its vault, fixed the stars, made the sun and moon revolve. The heaven without the least fissure (furūj) is the great visible argument: the perfection of the edifice attests the wisdom of the Architect, for whoever will raise his eyes.

mots.racine: ب ن ي / ز ي ن
mots.sens: how We built and adorned it
mots.def: banā: to build; zayyana: to adorn.

mots.racine: ف ر ج

versets.ar: وَالْأَرْضَ مَدَدْنَاهَا وَأَلْقَيْنَا فِيهَا رَوَاسِيَ وَأَنبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِن كُلِّ زَوْجٍ بَهِيجٍ
versets.tr: wa-l-arḍa madadnāhā wa-alqaynā fīhā rawāsiya wa-anbatnā fīhā min kulli zawjin bahījin
versets.trad: And the earth — We spread it out, cast firm mountains on it, and made grow in it every splendid kind,
versets.commentaire: To the vault of the sky answers the spreading of the earth: Qushayrī: We made it a cradle for them, gave it the mountains as pegs, made grow in it trees, flowers, and lights. Each “splendid pair” (zawj bahīj) of species is a sign: the teeming beauty of the created calls to recognize the Creator.

mots.racine: م د د
ز و ج / ب ه ج

versets.ar: تَبْصِرَةً وَذِكْرَىٰ لِكُلِّ عَبْدٍ مُّنِيبٍ
versets.tr: tabṣiratan wa-dhikrā li-kulli ʿabdin munībin
versets.trad: as an opening of insight and a reminder for every penitent servant.
versets.commentaire: All creation is tabṣira (an awakening of sight) and dhikrā (a reminder). Qushayrī: a sign and indication for whoever returns to Us (munīb), passing from the contemplation of Our acts to the vision of Our attributes, and from Our attributes to the contemplation of Our truth and essence. The degrees of unveiling: from acts to attributes, from attributes to the Essence.

mots.racine: ب ص ر / ذ ك ر
mots.def: tabṣira: awakening of sight; dhikrā: reminder.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ن و ب

versets.ar: وَنَزَّلْنَا مِنَ السَّمَاءِ مَاءً مُّبَارَكًا فَأَنبَتْنَا بِهِ جَنَّاتٍ وَحَبَّ الْحَصِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-nazzalnā mina s-samāʾi māʾan mubārakan fa-anbatnā bihi jannātin wa-ḥabba l-ḥaṣīdi
versets.trad: And We sent down from the sky blessed water, by which We made grow gardens and the grain that is harvested,
versets.commentaire: The blessed water (māʾ mubārak) — abundant in benefit. Qushayrī: by it We made grow gardens and the grain that is reaped; the components resemble one another, but their qualities — savours, scents, colours, forms, measures — differ. From the single water comes infinite diversity: one gift unfolds a thousand distinct blessings.

mots.racine: م و ه / ب ر ك
mots.def: mubārak: blessed, a source of blessing.

mots.racine: ح ب ب / ح ص د

versets.ar: وَالنَّخْلَ بَاسِقَاتٍ لَّهَا طَلْعٌ نَّضِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-n-nakhla bāsiqātin lahā ṭalʿun naḍīdun
versets.trad: and tall palm-trees with clustered spathes,
versets.commentaire: The lofty palms (bāsiqāt) with layered spathes (naḍīd). The towering height and the order of the clusters speak abundance arranged: God disposes the fruits in overload, one upon another, by profusion of His generosity. The verse runs over into the next — this nourishment is for the servants.

mots.racine: ن خ ل / ب س ق
ط ل ع / ن ض د

versets.ar: رِّزْقًا لِّلْعِبَادِ ۖ وَأَحْيَيْنَا بِهِ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ الْخُرُوجُ
versets.tr: rizqan lil-ʿibādi wa-aḥyaynā bihi baldatan maytan kadhālika l-khurūju
versets.trad: a provision for the servants. By it We revived a dead land. Such is the Resurrection!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: just as We drove this water to a land whose vegetation had withered, and as We did all these things being capable of them — so We will gather you at the Resurrection. Your raising is no farther than this. The dead land that grows green is the living proof of the “coming forth” (khurūj) from the graves: He who revives the soil revives the bodies.

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ع ب د
ح ي ي / ب ل د / م و ت
خ ر ج

versets.ar: كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ وَأَصْحَابُ الرَّسِّ وَثَمُودُ
versets.tr: kadhdhabat qablahum qawmu nūḥin wa-aṣḥābu r-rassi wa-thamūdu
versets.trad: Before them the people of Noah, the people of al-Rass, and Thamūd denied,
versets.commentaire: The enumeration of peoples annihilated for denying their prophets. Qushayrī sees in it a threat to these and a consolation for the Messenger: what struck the ancients lies in wait for the present, and the rejection of the Prophet is no new trial. The people of Noah, the people of al-Rass, the Thamūd — a chain of warnings.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب
mots.sens: before them the people of Noah denied
the people of al-Rass and Thamūd
mots.def: al-Rass: people of the well; Thamūd: people of Ṣāliḥ.

versets.ar: وَعَادٌ وَفِرْعَوْنُ وَإِخْوَانُ لُوطٍ
versets.tr: wa-ʿādun wa-firʿawnu wa-ikhwānu lūṭin
versets.trad: and ʿĀd, Pharaoh, and the brethren of Lot,
versets.commentaire: The list continues: ʿĀd (people of Hūd), Pharaoh, the “brethren of Lot” (the people of Sodom). Each was mighty, and each was swept away for treating the messengers as liars. The repetition of names hammers a single lesson: no power protects from the denial of the Truth.

mots.def: ʿĀd: people of Hūd; Firʿawn: Pharaoh.

mots.racine: أ خ و
mots.def: ikhwān Lūṭ: the people of Lot.

versets.ar: وَأَصْحَابُ الْأَيْكَةِ وَقَوْمُ تُبَّعٍ ۚ كُلٌّ كَذَّبَ الرُّسُلَ فَحَقَّ وَعِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-aṣḥābu l-aykati wa-qawmu tubbaʿin kullun kadhdhaba r-rusula fa-ḥaqqa waʿīdi
versets.trad: the people of al-Ayka, and the people of Tubbaʿ. All denied the messengers, so My threat came true.
versets.commentaire: The people of al-Ayka (Madyan) and the people of Tubbaʿ. The conclusion falls: all denied the messengers, and My threat (waʿīd) was fulfilled. Qushayrī: We were not powerless before those — the mention of their end is at once a threat to the deniers and a comfort to the Prophet. The promise of punishment is not vain.

mots.sens: the people of al-Ayka and of Tubbaʿ
mots.def: al-Ayka: Madyan; Tubbaʿ: a Himyarite king.

mots.racine: ك ذ ب / ح ق ق / و ع د
mots.sens: all denied, My threat came true

versets.ar: أَفَعَيِينَا بِالْخَلْقِ الْأَوَّلِ ۚ بَلْ هُمْ فِي لَبْسٍ مِّنْ خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ
versets.tr: a-fa-ʿayīnā bi-l-khalqi l-awwali bal hum fī labsin min khalqin jadīdin
versets.trad: Were We then wearied by the first creation? No! Yet they are in doubt about a new creation!
themes: the first and the second creation
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We were not powerless before the first creation — how should We be before the second, which is but the renewal? No act resisted Us, no thing wearied Us — how should the affair of resurrection be hard for Us? The argument is unanswerable: He who created the first time re-creates without toil. Their doubt (labs) is confusion, not reason.

mots.racine: ع ي ي / خ ل ق
mots.sens: were We wearied by the first creation?

mots.ar: بَلْ هُمْ فِي لَبْسٍ مِّنْ خَلْقٍ جَدِيدٍ
mots.tr: bal hum fī labsin min khalqin jadīd
mots.racine: ل ب س / خ ل ق
mots.sens: they are in doubt of a new creation

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنسَانَ وَنَعْلَمُ مَا تُوَسْوِسُ بِهِ نَفْسُهُ ۖ وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-laqad khalaqnā l-insāna wa-naʿlamu mā tuwaswisu bihi nafsuhu wa-naḥnu aqrabu ilayhi min ḥabli l-warīdi
versets.trad: We created man and We know what his soul whispers to him. We are nearer to him than his jugular vein.
versets.commentaire: The central verse of the sūra. Qushayrī: We know the whisperings of his soul — the cravings it seeks to satisfy, ostentation toward creatures, ill character, rancour, and all the soul's ills that trouble the heart and the moment. “We are nearer to him than his jugular vein”: by knowledge and power. And he adds, magnificent: in this verse there is dread and terror for some, and repose, calm, and intimacy of heart for others.

mots.racine: و س و س / ن ف س
mots.sens: what his soul whispers to him

mots.ar: وَنَحْنُ أَقْرَبُ إِلَيْهِ مِنْ حَبْلِ الْوَرِيدِ
mots.tr: wa-naḥnu aqrabu ilayhi min ḥabli l-warīd
mots.racine: ق ر ب / و ر د
mots.sens: We are nearer than his jugular vein

versets.ar: إِذْ يَتَلَقَّى الْمُتَلَقِّيَانِ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ وَعَنِ الشِّمَالِ قَعِيدٌ
versets.tr: idh yatalaqqā l-mutalaqqiyāni ʿani l-yamīni wa-ʿani sh-shimāli qaʿīdun
versets.trad: When the two recorders receive [his deeds], seated on the right and on the left,
versets.commentaire: Two angels gather (yatalaqqā) the deeds, one on the right, one on the left. Qushayrī reports a sweetness: he who records the evil today will, tomorrow, be the scribe of obedience; and it is said: those who record the faults change each day and each night, so that of your evils only a part be known, and the knowledge of your sins stay scattered among them. The very heavenly arrangement veils the sinner.

mots.racine: ل ق ي
ي م ن / ش م ل / ق ع د
mots.sens: on right and left a seated one

versets.ar: مَّا يَلْفِظُ مِن قَوْلٍ إِلَّا لَدَيْهِ رَقِيبٌ عَتِيدٌ
versets.tr: mā yalfiẓu min qawlin illā ladayhi raqībun ʿatīdun
versets.trad: He utters no word but there is by him a watcher ready to record.
versets.commentaire: No uttered word without a ready watcher (raqīb ʿatīd). The verse prolongs the dread of verse 16: not only does God know the whisperings of the heart, but every spoken word is seized at the instant. Awareness of this presence turns speech into an act of vigilance (murāqaba): one no longer speaks lightly under the gaze of the Witness.

mots.racine: ل ف ظ / ق و ل
ر ق ب / ع ت د
mots.sens: but by him is a ready watcher

versets.ar: وَجَاءَتْ سَكْرَةُ الْمَوْتِ بِالْحَقِّ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ مَا كُنتَ مِنْهُ تَحِيدُ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾat sakratu l-mawti bi-l-ḥaqqi dhālika mā kunta minhu taḥīdu
versets.trad: The intoxication of death comes in truth: that is what you sought to escape!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī meditates the states of the death-throes: when the soul is about to leave the world, the states differ: one sees his terror grow, and his fate is revealed only at the departure of the spirit; another receives an unveiling before he leaves, his dread is stilled, his reason is kept for him, his presence and discernment complete — he yields the spirit gently, without constraint or grimace. Death, dreaded, becomes for the saint the threshold of the encounter.

mots.racine: س ك ر / م و ت / ح ق ق
mots.sens: the intoxication of death comes in truth

mots.racine: ح ي د

versets.ar: وَنُفِخَ فِي الصُّورِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ الْوَعِيدِ
versets.tr: wa-nufikha fī ṣ-ṣūri dhālika yawmu l-waʿīdi
versets.trad: The trumpet is blown. That is the Day of the Threat!
versets.commentaire: The blast in the trumpet (ṣūr) opens the Day of the Threat (yawm al-waʿīd). What was only announcement becomes event: the threat promised to the past peoples (verse 14) is universalized. The trumpet's sound is the signal of the great gathering, where each soul will appear escorted.

mots.racine: ن ف خ / ص و ر
و ع د
mots.sens: that is the Day of the Threat

versets.ar: وَجَاءَتْ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّعَهَا سَائِقٌ وَشَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾat kullu nafsin maʿahā sāʾiqun wa-shahīdun
versets.trad: Each soul will come, with it a driver and a witness.
versets.commentaire: Each soul comes escorted by two angels. Qushayrī: a driver (sāʾiq) who leads it, either to Paradise or to the Fire, and a witness (shahīd) who testifies to what it did of good and evil. None appears alone at the rendezvous: the accomplished act becomes escort, and the secret of the heart finds its witness.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ن ف س
س و ق / ش ه د
mots.sens: with it a driver and a witness

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ كُنتَ فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ هَٰذَا فَكَشَفْنَا عَنكَ غِطَاءَكَ فَبَصَرُكَ الْيَوْمَ حَدِيدٌ
versets.tr: laqad kunta fī ghaflatin min hādhā fa-kashafnā ʿanka ghiṭāʾaka fa-baṣaruka l-yawma ḥadīdun
versets.trad: You were heedless of this; now We have lifted your veil, so your sight today is keen!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī turns the verse toward the believers here below: the believers, today, have keen sight (baṣar ḥadīd): they discern their right path and guard against their evil. To the disbeliever, tomorrow, these words are said as a reproach: the veil (ghiṭāʾ) falls too late. The lucidity refused in heedlessness (ghafla) then imposes itself, but can no longer serve.

mots.ar: لَّقَدْ كُنتَ فِي غَفْلَةٍ مِّنْ هَٰذَا
mots.tr: laqad kunta fī ghaflatin min hādhā
mots.racine: غ ف ل
ك ش ف / غ ط و
ب ص ر / ح د د

versets.ar: وَقَالَ قَرِينُهُ هَٰذَا مَا لَدَيَّ عَتِيدٌ
versets.tr: wa-qāla qarīnuhu hādhā mā ladayya ʿatīdun
versets.trad: His companion will say: “Here is what I have ready.”
versets.commentaire: The “companion” (qarīn) is here the recording angel: “Here is what I hold ready” — the register of deeds. Qushayrī: nothing of their states is hidden without being mentioned; if it is good, they are rewarded; if not, they are held accountable — either by mercy and pardon, or according to the measure of their fault. The file is complete, but mercy keeps the last word.

mots.racine: ق ر ن
mots.def: qarīn: companion (angel or devil per the verses).

mots.racine: ع ت د
mots.sens: here is what I have ready

versets.ar: أَلْقِيَا فِي جَهَنَّمَ كُلَّ كَفَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ
versets.tr: alqiyā fī jahannama kulla kaffārin ʿanīdin
versets.trad: You two, cast into Hell every obstinate disbeliever,
versets.commentaire: The order is given to the two angels (alqiyā, dual): cast every obstinate denier (kaffār ʿanīd). The denier is not merely the ignorant, but the one who stubbornly persists (ʿinād) against the evidence. The verse runs on into the portrait of this obstinate one, each trait redoubling his stubbornness.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ج ه ن م
ك ف ر / ع ن د

versets.ar: مَّنَّاعٍ لِّلْخَيْرِ مُعْتَدٍ مُّرِيبٍ
versets.tr: mannāʿin lil-khayri muʿtadin murībin
versets.trad: who withheld the good, a transgressor, a sower of doubt,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds “the withholder of good” (mannāʿ lil-khayr): he refuses the obligatory zakāt; he denies the Muslims the surplus of his water and pasture; he turns people from the good and from beneficence, speaking ill of one and another to make them loathe it; the withholder of good is the helper of evil. And “the sower of doubt” (murīb): he makes people doubt his case, for he is not sincere, and clouds his state, for he is a hypocrite.

mots.racine: م ن ع / خ ي ر
ع د و / ر ي ب
mots.def: muʿtadī: transgressor; murīb: who casts doubt.

versets.ar: الَّذِي جَعَلَ مَعَ اللَّهِ إِلَٰهًا آخَرَ فَأَلْقِيَاهُ فِي الْعَذَابِ الشَّدِيدِ
versets.tr: alladhī jaʿala maʿa llāhi ilāhan ākhara fa-alqiyāhu fī l-ʿadhābi sh-shadīdi
versets.trad: who set up another god beside God — so cast him into the severe punishment!
versets.commentaire: The height of obstinacy: to associate another deity with God (ilāh ākhar). It is the one sin nothing surpasses, the root of all others. The order closes: cast him into the severe punishment. Association (shirk) overturns the very order of existence, by attributing to another what belongs only to the One.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / أ ل ه
mots.sens: he set another god with God

mots.racine: ل ق ي / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: cast him into the severe punishment

versets.ar: قَالَ قَرِينُهُ رَبَّنَا مَا أَطْغَيْتُهُ وَلَٰكِن كَانَ فِي ضَلَالٍ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: qāla qarīnuhu rabbanā mā aṭghaytuhu wa-lākin kāna fī ḍalālin baʿīdin
versets.trad: His companion will say: “Our Lord, I did not make him transgress, but he was in profound error.”
versets.commentaire: Here the “companion” is the associated devil (qarīn). Qushayrī: when they meet in Hell, the devil says: I did not compel him to his unbelief; he did, by his own choice, what I suggested to him. The tempter slips away: he whispered, did not force. The “profound error” (ḍalāl baʿīd) remains the doing of the human choice, which suggestion only grazed.

mots.racine: ط غ ي
mots.sens: I did not make him transgress

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ع د
mots.sens: but he was in profound error

versets.ar: قَالَ لَا تَخْتَصِمُوا لَدَيَّ وَقَدْ قَدَّمْتُ إِلَيْكُم بِالْوَعِيدِ
versets.tr: qāla lā takhtaṣimū ladayya wa-qad qaddamtu ilaykum bi-l-waʿīdi
versets.trad: He will say: “Do not quarrel before Me! I had already sent you the threat.
versets.commentaire: “Do not dispute before Me”: the dispute of the tempted and the tempter is vain. Qushayrī: I had already commanded you uprightness and forbidden you error. The warning (waʿīd) had preceded the fault; none can plead ignorance. On the Day of reckoning, the casting-off of responsibility collapses before the Word given beforehand.

mots.racine: خ ص م
ق د م / و ع د
mots.sens: I preceded you with the threat

versets.ar: مَا يُبَدَّلُ الْقَوْلُ لَدَيَّ وَمَا أَنَا بِظَلَّامٍ لِّلْعَبِيدِ
versets.tr: mā yubaddalu l-qawlu ladayya wa-mā anā bi-ẓallāmin lil-ʿabīdi
versets.trad: The Word with Me does not change, and I am not unjust to the servants.”
versets.commentaire: “The Word with Me does not change” (mā yubaddalu l-qawl): what God has decreed comes about unfailingly. And yet: “I am not unjust (ẓallām) to the servants.” The immutability of the decree is no tyranny: none is punished but for what he chose, after warning. Divine justice and the fixity of His word meet.

mots.racine: ب د ل / ق و ل
mots.sens: the Word does not change with Me

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ع ب د
mots.sens: I am not unjust to the servants

versets.ar: يَوْمَ نَقُولُ لِجَهَنَّمَ هَلِ امْتَلَأْتِ وَتَقُولُ هَلْ مِن مَّزِيدٍ
versets.tr: yawma naqūlu li-jahannama hali mtalaʾti wa-taqūlu hal min mazīdin
versets.trad: On the Day We say to Hell, “Are you full?” and it says, “Are there yet more?”
versets.commentaire: A striking dialogue: “Are you full?” — “Are there yet more?” Qushayrī notes that this “saying” is figurative, for if Hell could answer, it would say so; in reality God gives it life so that it answers. Two readings of “hal min mazīd”: a call for more culprits, or an admission of saturation. God fills Hell with the deniers until, the believing sinners being drawn out, their fury increases yet more.

mots.racine: م ل أ
ز ي د

versets.ar: وَأُزْلِفَتِ الْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ
versets.tr: wa-uzlifati l-jannatu lil-muttaqīna ghayra baʿīdin
versets.trad: And the Garden is brought near to the godfearing, not far off:
versets.commentaire: Paradise is “brought near” (uzlifat) to the godfearing. Qushayrī distinguishes three classes: some are led to it on foot (the common believers); others mounted on their works figured as steeds (the elite); but the elite of the elite, of them it is said: “the Garden was brought near to the godfearing” — it comes to them. “Not far off” confirms the nearness; and it is also said: not far from the sinners, to comfort their hearts.

mots.racine: ز ل ف / ج ن ن / و ق ي
mots.sens: the Garden was brought near to the godfearing
mots.def: azlafa: to bring near; muttaqī: godfearing.

mots.racine: ب ع د

versets.ar: هَٰذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِكُلِّ أَوَّابٍ حَفِيظٍ
versets.tr: hādhā mā tūʿadūna li-kulli awwābin ḥafīẓin
versets.trad: “This is what you were promised, for every penitent keeper [of the covenant],
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī defines the two qualities: the awwāb is one who returns to God in all his states; the ḥafīẓ is one who guards his moments — and it is said: who guards his senses in God and watches over his breaths with God. Constant repentance (awb) and vigilance (ḥifẓ) are the two wings of the one who inherits the promise.

mots.racine: و ع د
mots.sens: this is what you are promised

mots.racine: أ و ب / ح ف ظ
mots.def: awwāb: who returns ceaselessly; ḥafīẓ: who guards.

versets.ar: مَّنْ خَشِيَ الرَّحْمَٰنَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَجَاءَ بِقَلْبٍ مُّنِيبٍ
versets.tr: man khashiya r-raḥmāna bi-l-ghaybi wa-jāʾa bi-qalbin munībin
versets.trad: who feared the Merciful in the Unseen and came with a penitent heart.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on “who feared the Merciful in the secret”: the fear of the Raḥmān is the fear of separation — it is joined to intimacy, which is why He did not say “who feared the Compeller” but “the Merciful.” And on “a penitent heart” (qalb munīb): He did not say “an obedient soul” but “a penitent heart,” so that there be in this a hope for the sinners: for even if they have failed by their souls, they have the bitterness of the heart and the sincerity of remorse.

mots.racine: خ ش ي / ر ح م / غ ي ب
mots.sens: who feared the Merciful in the secret

mots.racine: ق ل ب / ن و ب
mots.sens: and came with a penitent heart

versets.ar: ادْخُلُوهَا بِسَلَامٍ ۖ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُلُودِ
versets.tr: udkhulūhā bi-salāmin dhālika yawmu l-khulūdi
versets.trad: Enter it in peace: that is the Day of Eternity.”
versets.commentaire: “Enter it in peace” (bi-salām): Qushayrī specifies — in safety from every trial, and in the finding of good pleasure (riḍwān): never will the Real be angry with you. The “Day of Eternity” (yawm al-khulūd) is not only endless duration, but peace without return: felicity there is definitively safe from all alteration.

mots.racine: د خ ل / س ل م
خ ل د
mots.sens: that is the Day of Eternity

versets.ar: لَّهُم مَّا يَشَاءُونَ فِيهَا وَلَدَيْنَا مَزِيدٌ
versets.tr: lahum mā yashāʾūna fīhā wa-ladaynā mazīdun
versets.trad: They will have whatever they wish therein, and with Us is yet more.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī dwells on the precision: He did not say “what they ask” but “what they will” — all that passes through their mind is realized at once. And since, today, they say: “what God wills,” they will have tomorrow from Him beneficence. Is the reward of good anything but good? On “with Us is more” (ladaynā mazīd): the exegetes agree it is the vision (al-ruʾya), the gaze upon God — the gift beyond every gift.

mots.racine: ش ي أ
ز ي د

versets.ar: وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَشَدُّ مِنْهُم بَطْشًا فَنَقَّبُوا فِي الْبِلَادِ هَلْ مِن مَّحِيصٍ
versets.tr: wa-kam ahlaknā qablahum min qarnin hum ashaddu minhum baṭshan fa-naqqabū fī l-bilādi hal min maḥīṣin
versets.trad: How many a generation We destroyed before them, mightier than they! They roamed the lands; was there any escape?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: take a lesson from those before you: they sank into their error, persisted in it, did not pull free — so We destroyed them, leaving not one. Mightier (ashaddu baṭshan), they roamed the lands without finding an escape (maḥīṣ). Strength and mobility do not save from the decree: no refuge outside God.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن
ح ي ص

versets.ar: إِنَّ فِي ذَٰلِكَ لَذِكْرَىٰ لِمَن كَانَ لَهُ قَلْبٌ أَوْ أَلْقَى السَّمْعَ وَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ
versets.tr: inna fī dhālika la-dhikrā li-man kāna lahu qalbun aw alqā s-samʿa wa-huwa shahīdun
versets.trad: In that is a reminder for whoever has a heart, or lends an ear while being present as a witness.
versets.commentaire: A capital verse on the heart (qalb). Qushayrī: a present heart, turned toward beneficence, a heart not fickle; a wakeful heart that heedlessness does not intoxicate; a heart that counts its breaths with God, living by the light of accord. And he cites: “the heart is between two fingers of the Merciful”: between two graces — what He wards off of trial and what He bestows of blessings. The hadith of the vessels: the believer's heart is an intact vessel where faith enters and remains.

mots.racine: ق ل ب
ل ق ي / س م ع / ش ه د
mots.sens: or lends an ear as a present witness
mots.def: alqā l-samʿ: to lend an ear; shahīd: present.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ وَمَا مَسَّنَا مِن لُّغُوبٍ
versets.tr: wa-laqad khalaqnā s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa wa-mā baynahumā fī sittati ayyāmin wa-mā massanā min lughūbin
versets.trad: We created the heavens, the earth, and what is between them in six days, and no weariness touched Us.
versets.commentaire: A direct refutation of the denial of resurrection: the creation of the heavens and the earth caused Us no weariness (lughūb). Qushayrī: how would weariness touch Him, the Ṣamad, in whom no accident occurs? He whom the first creation did not exhaust cannot be hindered by the second. Weariness is proper to the created, not the Creator.

mots.racine: س ت ت / ي و م
م س س / ل غ ب

versets.ar: فَاصْبِرْ عَلَىٰ مَا يَقُولُونَ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّكَ قَبْلَ طُلُوعِ الشَّمْسِ وَقَبْلَ الْغُرُوبِ
versets.tr: fa-ṣbir ʿalā mā yaqūlūna wa-sabbiḥ bi-ḥamdi rabbika qabla ṭulūʿi sh-shamsi wa-qabla l-ghurūbi
versets.trad: So bear patiently what they say, and glorify the praise of your Lord before sunrise and before sunset;
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: if your hearing is wounded by what they say of Me — things from which My attribute is too holy —, be patient, and find repose in glorifying Me. The remedy for the wound of slander is tasbīḥ: to turn the heart toward the praise of God, morning and evening, dissolves the bitterness of men's scorn.

mots.racine: ص ب ر / ق و ل
mots.sens: be patient over what they say

mots.racine: س ب ح / ح م د
mots.sens: glorify the praise of your Lord

versets.ar: وَمِنَ اللَّيْلِ فَسَبِّحْهُ وَأَدْبَارَ السُّجُودِ
versets.tr: wa-mina l-layli fa-sabbiḥhu wa-adbāra s-sujūdi
versets.trad: and glorify Him in part of the night and after the prostration.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the night is the time of seclusion (khalwa) — and the purity in seclusion is fuller and more limpid. To glorify God by night, away from eyes, and after the prostration, is to seek intimacy in silence: the heart alone with its Lord finds a transparency the day does not give.

mots.racine: ل ي ل / س ب ح
mots.sens: part of the night, glorify Him

mots.racine: د ب ر / س ج د

versets.ar: وَاسْتَمِعْ يَوْمَ يُنَادِ الْمُنَادِ مِن مَّكَانٍ قَرِيبٍ
versets.tr: wa-stamiʿ yawma yunādi l-munādi min makānin qarībin
versets.trad: And listen for the Day when the caller will call from a near place,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the call comes from the Real and falls upon them; as the conclave takes place ceaselessly among men, the call that reaches them comes suddenly, with no choice for the servant in the act. The caller (munādī) calls “from a near place”: none will be able to claim he did not hear. The imposed listening of the Day answers the refusal to listen here below.

mots.racine: س م ع / ن د و
mots.sens: listen for the day the caller calls

mots.racine: ك و ن / ق ر ب

versets.ar: يَوْمَ يَسْمَعُونَ الصَّيْحَةَ بِالْحَقِّ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُرُوجِ
versets.tr: yawma yasmaʿūna ṣ-ṣayḥata bi-l-ḥaqqi dhālika yawmu l-khurūji
versets.trad: the Day they hear the Cry in truth: that is the Day of Coming Forth!
versets.commentaire: The Cry (ṣayḥa) “in truth” signals the coming forth (khurūj) from the graves — the resurrection promised since verse 11. What was compared to the revived land is accomplished: at the call, the dead surge up. The “Day of Coming Forth” closes the arc opened by “such is the coming forth”: the sūra fulfils its own prophecy.

mots.racine: س م ع / ص ي ح / ح ق ق
mots.sens: the day they hear the Cry in truth

mots.racine: خ ر ج
mots.sens: that is the Day of Coming Forth

versets.ar: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نُحْيِي وَنُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْنَا الْمَصِيرُ
versets.tr: innā naḥnu nuḥyī wa-numītu wa-ilaynā l-maṣīru
versets.trad: It is We who give life and give death, and to Us is the return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to Us the return of all and their becoming. The triple seal: God gives life, gives death, and all returns to Him. He who holds life and death holds also the resurrection; to deny the return is to ignore the One who disposes of both ends of existence. The maṣīr (becoming) brings all things back to their origin.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت
mots.sens: it is We who give life and death
mots.def: aḥyā: to vivify; amāta: to make die.

mots.racine: ص ي ر

versets.ar: يَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ الْأَرْضُ عَنْهُمْ سِرَاعًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ حَشْرٌ عَلَيْنَا يَسِيرٌ
versets.tr: yawma tashaqqaqu l-arḍu ʿanhum sirāʿan dhālika ḥashrun ʿalaynā yasīrun
versets.trad: The Day the earth splits open from them as they hasten forth: that is a gathering easy for Us.
versets.commentaire: The earth splits and the dead hasten (sirāʿ). Qushayrī: that is easy for Us, whether We created them all at once or one by one — and he cites: “your creation and your resurrection are but as that of a single soul.” The gathering (ḥashr) that seems inconceivable to men is “easy” (yasīr) for God: the multitude weighs no more than the one before His power.

mots.racine: ش ق ق / س ر ع
mots.sens: the day the earth splits, and they hasten

mots.racine: ح ش ر / ي س ر
mots.sens: that is a gathering easy for Us

versets.ar: نَّحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَا يَقُولُونَ ۖ وَمَا أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِجَبَّارٍ ۖ فَذَكِّرْ بِالْقُرْآنِ مَن يَخَافُ وَعِيدِ
versets.tr: naḥnu aʿlamu bimā yaqūlūna wa-mā anta ʿalayhim bi-jabbārin fa-dhakkir bi-l-qurʾāni man yakhāfu waʿīdi
versets.trad: We know best what they say, and you are not over them a tyrant. So remind by the Qurʾān whoever fears My threat.
versets.commentaire: The sūra closes on the Prophet's mission: you are not a tyrant (jabbār) who compels them. Qushayrī: dread, warning, and reminder act only on those who fear; as for the one who does not fear, intimidation profits him nothing — the bird of the sky alights only on its like. The reminder (dhikr) by the Qurʾān addresses the heart that can still tremble: faith is not imposed, it is awakened.

mots.racine: ج ب ر
mots.sens: you are not a tyrant over them

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / خ و ف / و ع د
mots.sens: remind by the Qurʾān whoever fears My threat
